titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph O'Carroll to Thomas Kirkpatrick, 1 October 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph Francis O'Carroll

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Joseph Francis O'Carroll (1856-1942) to Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick expressing his misgivings about his capabilities to preform as President of the College should Dr Bewley choose to defer his term.From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
43, MERRION SQUARE, DUBLIN. 1st Oct. 1916 My dear Registrar, I hoped very sincerely
that Dr. Bewley would take the Residency, to
which the votes of the College would have elected
him unanimously as against any other single
Fellow of the College. If however he desires to
defer his term of office, I feel that it is my
duty to the College, as it is a very great homour
to myself, to allow my name to go forward
as a candidate at the final election. But I
am so little confident of my ability to fill the
high position of President according to the Standards
set by the Presidents to whom I have known that
I should not feel at all displeased if some
more worthy candidate would be found in the
meantime. Yours sincerely Joseph O' Carroll T. Percy Kirkpatrick Esq MD. F.R.C.P.I. Registrar, R.C.P.
